QF.id: III-C Prevention of Money Laundering Act_27

question.text: Under the AML guidelines, records related to ongoing investigations or suspicious transaction reporting shall be retained until:

answer.text: Five years from the date of filing
Ten years from the date of transaction
It is confirmed that the case has been closed
The regulator issues a specific direction

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Section 9.3.7, where records relate to ongoing investigations or suspicious transaction reporting, they shall be retained until it is confirmed that the case has been closed. **The Trap:** The general 5-year retention period does not apply to records related to ongoing investigations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This could mean indefinite retention for cases that take years to resolve.
